This is a common phrase that the prophet sallall.aii wasallam used in his wording of very important matters and something really important would come up or something that he wished that the listeners would take particular note. He would use words this. So he swears by Allah subhanahu wa ta'ala that the dunya the life of this world will not cease to be until a man will cross a grave and find himself almost wrestling on top of the grave. in the Arabic language. And so what is meant here is that a person will pass by a grave and stop to appreciate and wish that he was in this grave that he was in the place of this person who is in the grave. and he will say either verbally or to himself, I wish that I was in this person's place in the grave. The prophet sallallah.aii wasallam then tells us why he is wishing for death or wishing that he had passed away or that he was in this grave. It is not because of anything except for the fitan the fitan or the fitna not being that he's having a hard physical life. His physical life is okay. There's no problem with his physical well-being. He's comfortable physically. The problem is is that he his dean and his character and his religion and his dutifulness to Allah. These things are being constantly challenged as the prophet sallall.aii alaihi wasallam tells us that at the end of the time a person holding to his religion, holding to what the prophet and his companions were upon will be as difficult and strenuous as holding on to a lit ember, a lit coal, and a person's hand, trying to hold on to it and keep hold of it. And so in this narration we have that this man is wishing for death. What grave is it that we're talking about? It's not the point. It's not any specific grave. Now does this mean that it is permissible for a person to ask for death? summary: This lecture discusses a hadith narration found in both Sahih Bukhari and Sahih Muslim regarding signs before the Day of Judgment. The speaker explains a hadith where the Prophet describes how people will wish for death due to the intense spiritual trials (fitna) affecting their religion and character near the end of time. The lecturer clarifies that while the hadith mentions people wishing for death, this doesn't necessarily permit asking for death during hardship, as that ruling is discussed elsewhere. The core message is about depicting the severity of spiritual corruption and difficulty in maintaining faith during the final times.
suggested_title: Signs Before the Day of Judgment: Understanding Hadith About Spiritual Trials and the End of Times
hadith_chapter: Book of signs before the Day of Judgment
catchy_title: When People Will Beg for Death
